summary: Backend (3/3 pytest) and Frontend (all routes & testids) verified for Life Portfolio. GET /api/, /api/status round-trip, and /api/resume/download all pass with correct PDF magic bytes, content-type and Content-Disposition filename. All routes (/, /professional, /personal, /emotional, /recruiter) render with required data-testids, expected counts (4 hobbies, 3 values, 2 projects, 3 education, 13 skills, 6 certs) and headings ('Data.|Clarity.|Initiative.', 'Warm.|Deep.|Human.'). World-switcher navigates correctly between dimensions and is hidden on /recruiter. Custom cursor correctly hidden on mobile (display:none).
observations: Emotional page renders 20 paragraphs (data-testid emotional-para-0..emotional-para-19). The review request mentioned 19 (para-0..para-18). This is a data/spec mismatch, not a defect — testids are consistent; one extra paragraph exists in /app/frontend/src/data/portfolio.js (line 132 'Yours — before you even knew it.'). Confirm whether intentional.

Resume download anchors (download-resume-btn, recruiter-download-btn) have no HTML `download` attribute, but the backend sets `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="Hemanth_Naga_Sai_Chakka_Resume.pdf"`, so browsers will trigger a download. Working as expected.
Custom cursor elements (.cursor-dot, .cursor-ring) correctly use display:none on viewport <=768px.
action_items: Optional: Reconcile emotional paragraph count — either update spec to 20 or remove the extra paragraph in /app/frontend/src/data/portfolio.js (current entries at lines 113-132).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: textTransform='uppercase' is not a valid ParagraphStyle attribute in ReportLab; it is silently ignored. Section headings are already typed in uppercase strings ('SUMMARY', 'EXPERIENCE'...) so output is fine, but the attribute is dead code and should be removed.
server.py: GET /api/status converts isoformat strings back to datetime in a Python loop; for larger datasets prefer projecting/transforming in Mongo or storing native datetimes consistently.
server.py: deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); migrate to lifespan context manager.
Frontend: download-resume-btn and recruiter-download-btn could add a `download` attribute for clarity even though Content-Disposition handles it.
Frontend: data file has 20 emotional paragraphs while spec says 19 — align.
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST_pytest_client status_check via POST /api/status during round-trip test (acceptable test residue; status_checks is a non-user-facing diagnostic collection).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_life_portfolio_api.py covers root, resume PDF (magic bytes + Content-Disposition filename), and status round-trip. Run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Frontend testids documented in review_request match implementation. Emotional has 20 paragraphs (para-0..para-19), not 19. Resume download relies on Content-Disposition header from backend, not HTML download attribute.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Backend and frontend integration verified end-to-end.
